behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure is at a local module boundary (change-parser output handed to report aggregator), not just one isolated function. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess is required to reproduce the core bug; a deterministic in-process fixture of parsed git changes including rename entries is sufficient. INTEGRATION-BRAKE: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required for this specific rule, (b) no AI/ML output, (c) no multi-agent chain, (d) no LLM handoff. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered, but disqualified by behavior text focusing on categorization semantics ("own category" and "not also counted") rather than CLI/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire parser + categorizer + report-builder in-process. Feed a mixed change set containing rename records and assert report counts `renamed=1`, `added=0`, `deleted=0` for that file, and that the renamed path appears only in the rename section.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this failure commonly depends on real git diff/subprocess output and byte/encoding behavior for binary patches. MOCK-HIDE: string-based fakes often miss decode/pipe edge cases and would falsely pass. No LLM dependency and no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit baseline, add/modify a true binary file, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert non-zero crash does not occur (exit code success) and binary file is included in report counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: unborn-HEAD behavior is a real git/CLI edge case (exit codes/stdout-stderr semantics) that appears when talking to actual repo state and subprocesses. MOCK-HIDE: mocked git responses can hide real command failure modes. No AI/ML or agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize an empty git repo with no commits, invoke the CLI subprocess against it, and assert successful execution plus structurally empty report output (no crash, no stack trace).

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: validating against actual repository history requires real git state transitions over commits/index/worktree. MOCK-HIDE: mocked history risks asserting against the same assumptions as implementation and missing divergence from real git semantics. No AI/ML or multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known numbers of adds/deletes/renames/modifies across several commits, run the CLI, and compare emitted counts to independently computed expected counts from git commands or a trusted oracle script.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: staged vs unstaged correctness depends on real git index/worktree semantics and command invocation behavior. MOCK-HIDE: faking staged/unstaged states can miss real index interactions and flag handling bugs. No AI/ML dependency and no agentic multi-step flow.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as unstaged and once staged, run the CLI subprocess in both states, and assert byte-for-byte identical normalized structured output.
